_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/<condition>/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
min_doctor_qualification: MS/ DNB/ PG Diploma or equivalent (in ENT)
MCh. / DNB or equivalent (in Pediatric Surgery)
clinical_indications: Deteriorating clinical features despite parenteral antibiotics for 24-48 hours

preauth.label: Clinical notes (detailing signs, symptoms, examination findings, indications for doing the procedure & advice for admission)
Clinical photograph of the affected part
Contrast enhanced CT/ lateral soft tissue neck X-ray supporting the diagnosis/ USG-of neck

claim.label: Post procedure clinical photograph of the affected part
Culture/ sensitivity report of the pus removed

ppd_preauth.q: Was the detailed Clinical notes – all vitals, detailed history especially previous surgery, symptoms, signs, physical examination including local examination, indication for procedure, advice for admission, and planned line of treatment submitted?
Was the Contrast enhanced CT/ lateral soft tissue neck X-ray supporting the diagnosis/ USG of neck investigation report confirming the diagnosis submitted?
Was the Clinical photograph of the affected part submitted?

cpd_claim.q: Are the detailed Indoor case papers with daily vitals and treatment details submitted?
Was Detailed Procedure / Operative Notes submitted?
Was the Post procedure clinical photograph submitted?
Was the culture/ sensitivity report of the pus report submitted?
Was the Detailed discharge summary submitted?

common_queries: Clinical notes or imaging reports not indicative of surgery for deep neck abscess drainage.
Missing clinical notes at pre-authorization for deep neck abscess drainage.
Missing clinical photograph of the affected part at pre-authorization for deep neck abscess drainage.
Missing contrast enhanced CT/ lateral soft tissue neck X-ray or USG of neck report at pre-authorization for deep neck abscess drainage.
Missing culture/ sensitivity report of the pus removed at claim submission for deep neck abscess drainage.

clinical_key_pointers: Proceed for surgery only if diagnosis is backed by clinical signs, symptoms, examination.

Deep neck abscess drainage is the surgical procedure intended for drainage of purulent collection and removal of devitalized tissue in the background of a suspected case of deep neck space abscess or necrotizing fasciitis.

Deep neck space infections are rapidly spreading, mostly polymicrobial infections along the fascial planes and involving the spaces in head neck region. The most common source of infection is dental origin.

Common deep neck space infections are- Ludwig's angina (submandibular space abscess), Quinsy (peritonsillar space abscess) and parapharyngeal space abscess. These infections may also spread to retropharyngeal space, parotid space, masseteric space, temporal space, and carotid space.
Necrotizing fibrofascitis is a potentially fatal infection caused by group A Streptococcus and characterized by involvement of skin, subcutaneous tissue and muscles.

Presentation- Acute onset pain, fever, swelling, trismus, dysphagia. Skin necrosis, subcutaneous crepitus and blisters suggest necrotizing fibrofascitis. Underlying immunocompromised conditions may mask or subdue the presenting features.
additional_information.pre_op_preparation: Microbiological evaluation: culture and sensitivity study of the drained material/ debrided tissue must be considered for gram positive, gram negative and anaerobes (whenever feasible) to guide the antibiotic medications.
additional_information.comorbidity_management: Underlying immunocompromised conditions may mask or subdue the presenting features.
